<<THE RESEARCH ASSISTANT IS DESIGNING AN EXPERIMENT TO TEST THE EFFECTIVENESS OF A NEW DRUG ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE>>  

[THE ASSISTANT COLLECTS DATA FROM A SAMPLE OF 50 PATIENTS RECORDING THEIR BLOOD PRESSURE BEFORE AND AFTER ADMINISTERING THE DRUG]  

[THE DATA COLLECTED IS AS FOLLOWS]  

[PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURE: X1, X2, ..., X50]  

[POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURE: Y1, Y2, ..., Y50]  

[ASSUME THAT THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES XI ARE NORMALLY DISTRIBUTED WITH MEAN MU_X AND VARIANCE SIGMA_X2 SQUARE]  

[THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES YI ARE NORMALLY DISTRIBUTED WITH MEAN MU_Y AND VARIANCE SIGMA_Y2 SQUARE]  

[LET THE COVARIANCE BETWEEN XI AND YI BE SIGMA_XY]  

[DERIVE THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS (MLEs) FOR THE PARAMETERS MU_X, MU_Y, SIGMA_X2 SQUARE, SIGMA_Y2 SQUARE, AND SIGMA_XY GIVEN THE DATA]  

[TO TEST THE HYPOTHESIS THAT THE DRUG HAS A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE FORMULATE AND CONDUCT A HYPOTHESIS TEST USING THE DERIVED MLEs]  

[STATE THE NULL AND ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS, THE TEST STATISTIC, AND THE REJECTION CRITERIA AT A SIGNIFICANCE LEVEL OF ALPHA EQUAL TO 0.05]  

[THE NULL HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG HAS NO EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE]  

[THE ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG DOES HAVE A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE]  

[THE TEST STATISTIC IS THE F-RATIO OR THE T-RATIO]  

[THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE Critical Value AT ALPHA EQUAL TO 0.05]  

[THE RESPONSE MUST CONTAIN AT LEAST 8 PLACEHOLDERS REPRESENTED BY SQUARE BRACKETS SUCH AS ADDRESS]  

[THE RESPONSE MUST CONTAIN EVERY WORD IN THE RESPONSE WRAPPED IN SQUARE BRACKETS]
